segments.text: Let brotherly love continue.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Be not forgetful to entertain strangers: for thereby some have entertained angels unawares.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Remember them that are in bonds, as bound with them; and them which suffer adversity, as being yourselves also in the body.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Marriage is honourable in all, and the bed undefiled: but whoremongers and adulterers God will judge.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Let your conversation be without covetousness; and be content with such things as ye have: for he hath said, I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So that we may boldly say, The Lord is my helper, and I will not fear what man shall do unto me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Remember them which have the rule over you, who have spoken unto you the word of God: whose faith follow, considering the end of their conversation.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Jesus Christ the same yesterday, and to day, and for ever.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Be not carried about with divers and strange doctrines. For it is a good thing that the heart be established with grace; not with meats, which have not profited them that have been occupied therein.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: We have an altar, whereof they have no right to eat which serve the tabernacle.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For the bodies of those beasts, whose blood is brought into the sanctuary by the high priest for sin, are burned without the camp.
Wherefore Jesus also, that he might sanctify the people with his own blood, suffered without the gate.
Let us go forth therefore unto him without the camp, bearing his reproach.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For here have we no continuing city, but we seek one to come.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: By him therefore let us offer the sacrifice of praise to God continually, that is, the fruit of our lips giving thanks to his name.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But to do good and to communicate forget not: for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Obey them that have the rule over you, and submit yourselves: for they watch for your souls, as they that must give account, that they may do it with joy, and not with grief: for that is unprofitable for you.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Pray for us: for we trust we have a good conscience, in all things willing to live honestly.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But I beseech you the rather to do this, that I may be restored to you the sooner.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Now the God of peace, that brought again from the dead our Lord Jesus, that great shepherd of the sheep, through the blood of the everlasting covenant,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Make you perfect in every good work to do his will, working in you that which is wellpleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ; to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And I beseech you, brethren, suffer the word of exhortation: for I have written a letter unto you in few words.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Know ye that our brother Timothy is set at liberty; with whom, if he come shortly, I will see you.
Salute all them that have the rule over you, and all the saints. They of Italy salute you.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Grace be with you all. Amen.
segments.speaker: Paul
